intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely invalidated by the visual evidence. P1, P2, and P4 are not identifiable as living specimens. P3 (Pothos) is present but in a state of total structural collapse/necrosis.

plant_audit.P1: No visible plant matter. Soil surface appears undisturbed but devoid of life.
plant_audit.P2: No viable foliage. Only desiccated organic debris remains.
plant_audit.P3: The Pothos specimen has undergone complete postural collapse. The white rabbit anchor is present, but the plant is non-viable.
plant_audit.P4: Merged with P2, showing no signs of active growth or chlorophyll retention.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Dry, compacted, and showing signs of long-term neglect.
biome_observations.fungal_presence: None detected, likely due to extreme desiccation.
biome_observations.debris: White rabbit anchor is present in P3; plastic sensor tags are present in P1/P2/P4.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: Stagnant state; no growth, continued decline of existing necrotic tissue.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_CURRENT: Total loss of visual data (blackout/sensor failure) followed by a final state of complete biological failure.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of total biological collapse. No living plant tissue is visible in any of the pots. The environment is hostile to the registered species.
vision_report.anomalies: The transition from T-2 to T-1 shows a complete sensor/camera blackout, followed by a grey-scale image at T-1 and a total black image at CURRENT, indicating a failure of the monitoring system or the environment.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic failure of the indoor botanical environment. The specimens have progressed from a state of severe stress to total necrosis and eventual disappearance from the visual field. The presence of the white rabbit anchor in a pot devoid of healthy Pothos confirms the loss of the primary specimen. The current state is one of complete botanical expiration.
